summary: Iteration 8 — Comprehensive testing of unified lead capture across Manual Entry, CSV Upload, Facebook Lead Ads webhook (Meta verify + HMAC) and Google Ads webhook. 17/17 new backend tests pass, 41/41 regression tests (saas_billing + multi_industry) pass. Frontend Manual Add flow + CSV controls + Integrations page render correctly for the Education demo tenant.

ui_bugs.issue: Card shows 'Not connected' even when integrations.google_ads.webhook_secret is set on the org. Backend webhook auth uses webhook_secret and is fully functional; the UI 'connected' badge appears to be keyed on a different field (e.g. customer_id / conversion_action_id) and is misleading. Either change the badge to read webhook_secret presence, or label the secret-only state as 'Webhook only'.
ui_bugs.selector: Integrations → Google Ads card status badge

action_items: OPTIONAL: Update Google Ads integration card 'Connected' badge logic to reflect webhook_secret presence (or rename status to 'Webhook configured').

OPTIONAL: Consider also testing a non-Education industry tenant (IT / Real Estate / Travel) end-to-end so the dynamic extras (company_name / budget_range / preferred_date / travellers) render on the Manual Add dialog. Current Education tenant correctly suppresses extras (course_interested is rendered as the main course select).
critical_code_review_comments: FB webhook payload signature is verified AFTER resolving tenant by page_id, which means an attacker hitting an unknown page_id receives a 200 OK with imported=0 without ever exposing the signature check. That is acceptable (avoids leaking which page_ids are configured) but worth a comment in code.

_ingest_external_lead uses partial unique index on (organization_id, source_external_id) — confirmed creates idempotent behaviour. Good. Duplicate response returns lead_id:null which the test correctly asserts.

FB Graph API call uses a real outbound HTTP request — failure is swallowed and lead is just not created (imported=0). For production, recommend recording the failure in an integration_errors log so admins can debug invalid page_access_token.

LeadsPage.jsx — formConfig fetch has a silent .catch(() => {}). If form-config endpoint ever fails, the dialog silently renders without industry extras with no user feedback. Consider a toast or warning.
server.py is 4245 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Should be split (leads, integrations, billing modules).
Google Ads endpoint accepts both flat JSON and native user_column_data — both code paths tested and working.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, leads add, lead list, integrations page)

test_credentials: Super Admin: admin@educationcrm.com / Admin@123 ; Org Admin: orgadmin@demo.com / Demo@123 (httpOnly cookie auth — Bearer not used; pytest uses requests.Session)
seed_data_creation: Test created ~6 leads in demo org: TEST_CSV_A/B/C_*, TEST_MAN_*, TEST_GAds_Flat_*, TEST_GAds_Native_*, TEST_FE_* — all in demo org (orgadmin@demo.com). No cleanup performed (tags are uuid-suffixed, no collisions).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All five lead capture channels (Manual, CSV, Widget shared schema, Facebook webhook, Google Ads webhook) are working. Auth uses httpOnly cookies — use requests.Session() in pytest (NOT Authorization Bearer headers). Backend URL from /app/frontend/.env (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). FB page_access_token is FAKE so FB webhook returns imported=0 by design — do not assert FB lead creation. Google Ads + Manual + CSV all create real leads. Demo org subscription status uses key 'status' (values: trial/active), not 'subscription_status'. Idempotency works via partial unique index on (organization_id, source_external_id).
rca of the issue: N/A — no production bugs found. One initial test failure was due to my test reusing static mobile numbers 9990000001-3 from a prior run that the backend's duplicate-detection correctly skipped — fixed by uuid+timestamp-suffixing the mobiles.
